title: Evaluating Gender Bias in Natural Language Inference

structured.summary: The paper investigates gender bias in Natural Language Inference (NLI) models by introducing a challenge task using gender-neutral premises paired with gender-swapped hypotheses, revealing stereotypical gender biases in models like BERT, RoBERTa, and BART. It proposes a simple data augmentation debiasing method via gender swapping in training data that reduces bias while preserving task accuracy.

strengths.point: Novel evaluation methodology that isolates gender bias in NLI models
strengths.evidence: Constructed challenge task with gender-neutral premises and gender-swapped hypotheses to detect bias in model predictions.

strengths.point: Comprehensive empirical analysis across multiple pretrained Transformer models
strengths.evidence: Evaluated BERT, RoBERTa, and BART on in-distribution and out-of-distribution datasets, showing consistent gender bias linked to occupational stereotypes.

strengths.point: Simple and effective debiasing approach via gender-swapping data augmentation
strengths.evidence: Augmentation reduces stereotypical bias metrics (ΔP, B) without degrading accuracy, especially for BERT.

weaknesses.point: Lack of comparison with other established bias mitigation techniques
weaknesses.evidence: No experimental comparison to adversarial training, INLP, or other debiasing methods leaves novelty and effectiveness relative to prior work unclear.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient detail and validation on gender-neutral premise construction
weaknesses.evidence: No linguistic or human validation to confirm premises are truly free of subtle gender cues, potentially confounding bias measurements.

weaknesses.point: Evaluation limited to binary gender and occupational domain
weaknesses.evidence: Metrics and datasets consider only male/female pairs and occupations, ignoring non-binary genders and other bias types such as intersectionality.

weaknesses.point: Missing statistical significance testing and ablation studies
weaknesses.evidence: Results lack confidence intervals or significance tests; no ablation on augmentation size or template sensitivity.

weaknesses.point: Reproducibility concerns due to incomplete methodological details
weaknesses.evidence: No full description of gender-swapping dictionary, nor model training hyperparameters, hindering exact replication.

weaknesses.point: Challenge dataset may lack ecological validity
weaknesses.evidence: Artificial construction with strictly gender-swapped hypotheses may not reflect typical NLI usage or downstream scenarios.

questions.question: How does the proposed data augmentation compare quantitatively to other debiasing techniques?
questions.motivation: To establish if the method is state-of-the-art or complementary and understand trade-offs.

questions.question: To what extent do subtle gender cues remain in the gender-neutral premises, and how do these affect bias measurements?
questions.motivation: Accurate bias quantification requires confidence in premise neutrality.

questions.question: Can the approach generalize beyond English and occupation-related gender bias to other languages and bias types?
questions.motivation: Broader applicability is critical for real-world impact.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of comparison with other established bias mitigation techniques. No experimental comparison to adversarial training, INLP, or other debiasing methods leaves novelty and effectiveness relative to prior work unclear
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient detail and validation on gender-neutral premise construction. No linguistic or human validation to confirm premises are truly free of subtle gender cues, potentially confounding bias measurements
critique_points.point_003: Evaluation limited to binary gender and occupational domain. Metrics and datasets consider only male/female pairs and occupations, ignoring non-binary genders and other bias types such as intersectionality
critique_points.point_004: Missing statistical significance testing and ablation studies. Results lack confidence intervals or significance tests; no ablation on augmentation size or template sensitivity
critique_points.point_005: Reproducibility concerns due to incomplete methodological details. No full description of gender-swapping dictionary, nor model training hyperparameters, hindering exact replication
critique_points.point_006: Challenge dataset may lack ecological validity. Artificial construction with strictly gender-swapped hypotheses may not reflect typical NLI usage or downstream scenarios
